text:
Young gymnasts in the Chicago are gathered to watch Simone Biles and Team USA compete Thursday in the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, as they aspire to be Olympians one day. NBC Chicago’s JC Navarrete reports.

Suni Lee gave it her best shot at defending her women's all-around gold medal on Thursday, and while Simone Biles ended up capturing the gold, Lee walked out of the Olympic event with a bronze medal.

Lee, who qualified for the all-around competition on the strength of a great qualification round in the team event, ended up winning the bronze medal thanks to her 14.866 on the uneven bars and an even 14.000 on the balance beam.

Stream NBC 5 for free, 24/7, wherever you are.

She finished with a score of 56.465, with Simone Biles winning the event at 59.131.

Here are the best highlights of her routines in Paris.

Lee’s beam routine gave her one of the best scores of the night and helped her position herself for her bronze medal run.

Lee’s 14.866 on the uneven bars was the second-best score of the evening in the event, with Kaylia Nemour’s 15.533 blowing away the rest of the field.

Even still, it positioned Lee to compete for the bronze medal.



Lee also had tremendous evenings on the floor exercise and the vault. You can watch the rest of the event here:
title: Watch Suni Lee’s bronze medal-winning Olympics run – NBC Chicago
